exhibit.title: The Resurrection
exhibit.intro: Touch a glowing point on the painting to learn about that detail. Carl Bloch's Resurrection shows the risen Christ ascending from the tomb, flanked by two adoring angels.

regions.title: The Halo of Light
regions.text: A radiant golden halo crowns Christ's head, the painter's way of rendering divine glory as physical light. Bloch dissolves the edges of the halo into the dim cave, so holiness appears to emanate outward rather than sit as a flat disc.

regions.title: The Face of the Risen Christ
regions.text: The upturned face is serene rather than triumphant. Bloch studied Danish models in natural light, giving the features a tender humanity that distinguishes his Christ from earlier, more austere depictions.

regions.title: Hair and Beard
regions.text: Loose strands of hair and a softly painted beard catch the halo's light. The warm browns are built from thin glazes, a technique Bloch inherited from the Dutch masters he admired.

regions.title: The Raised Right Hand
regions.text: Christ's right hand opens toward heaven. Look closely for the faint wound of the crucifixion at the wrist — a quiet reminder that the glorified body still bears the marks of the Passion.

regions.title: The Raised Left Hand
regions.text: The mirrored left hand completes the gesture of ascent. The arms form a wide V that draws the visitor's eye upward, out of the dark tomb and toward the unseen light above the frame.

regions.title: The White Burial Garment
regions.text: The flowing white robe is both burial shroud and garment of glory. Bloch renders dozens of fold-shadows in cool grey-violet, making the cloth the brightest mass in the composition.

regions.title: The Praying Angel
regions.text: Kneeling at the lower left, this angel presses its hands together in adoration. Its gaze lifts toward Christ, guiding the visitor's attention back to the central figure.

regions.title: The Witnessing Angel
regions.text: The second angel turns inward, framing the scene on the right. Its great folded wing is painted in soft ivory tones that echo Christ's robe, binding the three figures together.

regions.title: The White Lilies
regions.text: A cluster of white lilies blooms at Christ's feet — a traditional emblem of purity and resurrection. Their presence inside a tomb signals new life breaking through death.

regions.title: The Tomb and the Rock
regions.text: The dark cavern of the sepulchre swallows the background in browns and blacks. By keeping the tomb in shadow, Bloch makes the luminous figure of Christ read as light conquering darkness.

regions.title: The Discarded Armor
regions.text: A helmet and weapons lie abandoned in the lower left — the gear of the Roman guards set to watch the tomb. Their scattered arms testify that no earthly power could hold the grave shut.

regions.title: The Broken Stone
regions.text: A cracked stone slab lies across the foreground. Once sealing the tomb, it is now split and cast aside, its hard diagonal lines contrasting with the soft drapery above.
